claims: Ion transport matrix elements versus collisionality at Er=0
High-collisionality trend should agree with the SFINCS 2014 figure family
acceptance_gates: Regenerate the full LHD collisionality scan from the fixed scan-input writer
Emit a machine-readable summary with collision-operator labels, collisionality values, and transport matrices
Verify FP/PAS separation and high-collisionality trend assertions before promoting the figure to publication grade

claims: The corrected bounded LHD collisionality lane resolves clear FP/PAS separation again
This branch artifact is suitable for regression and manuscript-layout work, but the full collisionality figure family still needs regenerated audited artifacts
acceptance_gates: Summary artifact contains both Fokker-Planck and PAS labels on the pinned four-point collisionality ladder
For every pinned collisionality, PAS diagonal transport coefficients remain separated from FP coefficients
Figure and JSON artifacts stay checked in until the full-resolution lane replaces this bounded scaffold

claims: Ion transport matrix elements versus collisionality at Er=0

Emit a machine-readable summary with collision-operator labels, collisionality values, and transport matrices
Verify FP/PAS separation and operator-ordering assertions before promoting the figure to publication grade

claims: The corrected bounded W7-X collisionality lane resolves clear FP/PAS separation again
This branch artifact is suitable for regression and manuscript-layout work, but the full collisionality figure family still needs regenerated audited artifacts
acceptance_gates: Summary artifact contains both Fokker-Planck and PAS labels on the pinned four-point collisionality ladder

claims: High-collisionality transport-matrix trends should agree with analytic asymptotic limits
acceptance_gates: Regenerate only after the parent LHD and W7-X collisionality scans are rebuilt from corrected summaries
Record the asymptotic normalization used for the Simakov-Helander comparison
Check the high-collisionality trend numerically from the JSON summary, not only visually from the plot

claims: The three trajectory models agree at Er=0 on the fixed tokamak-like reference input
Away from Er=0, the tokamak-like reference sweep resolves clear separation in fluxes and flows across DKES, partial, and full trajectories
acceptance_gates: Pinned summary contains DKES, partial, and full trajectory models at negative, zero, and positive Er
All trajectory models agree at Er=0 within the strict pinned tolerance
Finite-Er records resolve model separation in at least one flux or flow diagnostic

claims: The three trajectory models agree at Er=0 on the fixed stellarator-like scaffold input
A bounded fast stellarator-like sweep resolves nonzero-Er separation while the full-resolution version remains a heavier validation lane
acceptance_gates: Pinned summary contains DKES, partial, and full trajectory models at negative, zero, and positive Er
All trajectory models agree at Er=0 within the strict pinned tolerance
Finite-Er records resolve nonzero bootstrap-current or flow spread before this scaffold is used as a manuscript figure

claims: Ambipolar Er trends and neoclassical heat-flux ordering should follow the W7-X validation literature
acceptance_gates: Document the exact VMEC/Boozer/profile inputs used for the comparison
Solve a finite ambipolar-Er root with bracket/provenance stored in the artifact
Compare heat-flux ordering and Er sign/magnitude trend against the published W7-X validation context without claiming experimental agreement beyond the reconstructed inputs

claims: Monoenergetic coefficients and low-collisionality trends should overlap with MONKES on shared model subsets
KNOSOS-style low-collisionality ordering should be respected when exact model overlap is not available
acceptance_gates: Separate exact shared-model comparisons from qualitative low-collisionality trend checks
Pin all compared coefficients, normalizations, and geometry inputs in machine-readable artifacts
Use MONKES/KNOSOS only as cross-code evidence where the modeled equations and normalizations are explicitly aligned

claims: Directional derivatives from autodiff should agree with finite differences and implicit-diff checks
Geometry-sensitivity maps should be stable enough for optimization-oriented workflows
acceptance_gates: Directional derivatives agree with centered finite differences on pinned scalar objectives
Implicit-diff gradients remain finite and residual-clean for both GMRES and BiCGStab wrappers
Optimization examples keep optional dependencies explicit and do not change production solver defaults
